name: Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic (1935–1936)

colors.symbolism: the hammer and sickle and the inscription

description: A red field with a gold hammer and sickle in the upper hoist, beneath which the name of the republic is inscribed in gold in the Latin Tajik script ('ÇSS Toçikiston').
history: Established by a decree of the Presidium of the Tajik Central Committee dated 4 July 1935: a red flag with a gold sickle and hammer in the upper hoist over the Romanised Tajik inscription 'ÇSS Toçikiston'. It was superseded on 26 September 1936 when a Cyrillic inscription was added.

features.symbolism: the union of industrial workers and peasants
the name of the Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic
